steps.title: Use perpendicular coefficients
steps.content:
For lines $Ax+By=C$ and $Dx+Ey=F$, perpendicular lines have orthogonal normal vectors, so
$$AD+BE=0$$
For the given pair, this gives
$$5a+7b=0$$

steps.title: Test the scaled pair
steps.content:
For the pair $10x+7y=1$ and $ax+2by=1$, the perpendicular condition is
$$(10)(a)+(7)(2b)=10a+14b$$
$$10a+14b=2(5a+7b)=0$$
So this pair is also perpendicular. The answer is B.
